evidence_quotes.quote: specific games being blocked from purchase on services like Steam and itch.io
forcing Riot games to moderate chats during official streams to remove any negative sentiment against Visa & Mastercard or risk losing their partnership with these companies.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I'm writing regarding the recent overstepping made by Mastercard and Visa and their blatant abuse of their monopoly over payment processing. As I'm sure you know of the recent news regarding specific games being blocked from purchase on services like Steam and itch.io, as well as forcing Riot games to moderate chats during official streams to remove any negative sentiment against Visa & Mastercard or risk losing their partnership with these companies. This is an EXTREMELY problematic situation where private companies have the power to censor and regulate other business and free speech simply because they have such a large marketshare and are willing to abuse their power as a result. A payment processor should never have the ability to decide what goods and services can be bought through them in an economy like this where that power can be so easily abused and used for nefarious purposes, which is the current case. It's especially egregious since these companies are not even based in the EU, allowing american companies this level of control over the EU market is an extreme security risk and can easily escalate to the censoring of important information regarding the preservation of democracy and our freedoms in the EU, as well as infringing on our right to free speech and freedom of expression. As I see it, the EU is failing to protect all EU citizens privacy and rights, especially if we bring the repeated attempts by the EU to pass Chat Control and the blatant overreach made recently by the UK and American company Google to ban vast amounts of content rated 18+ on the internet and Youtube, respectively, which is nothing more than a blatant attempt to gain access to private citizens ID cards. Which is an extreme risk in itself considering databases get hacked constantly and getting vast amounts of private citizens ID cards in the hands of hackers is only a matter of time at this point. My ask from the EU is to start taking EU citizens rights on the internet a lot more seriously than is currently shown. This is also a huge display of weakness on the geopolitical world stage as this shows to our adversaries that we don't have control over our own region and information services operating within, as well as basically waving our citizens private ID cards and private conversations for everyone to see, daring our adversaries to hack into our databases and gain access to the private information. I am deeply disappointed in the EU regarding the current state of these topics and hope that you start showing respect for your citizens and stop treating us like idiots with no right to privacy, as well as carelessly giving power to adversarial organizations to control the EU populations rights to purchase legal goods and services. Thank you for your time, Victor Fogelström
